channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: President-elect Donald Trump tries again to get Friday's hush money sentencing called off
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump tried again Tuesday to delay this week’s sentencing in his hush money case, asking a New York appeals court to intervene as he fights to avoid the finality of his conviction before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Girl Scouts discontinuing 2 cookie varieties after 2025 season
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 bodies are found in the landing gear of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Two bodies were found in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue aircraft at a South Florida airport, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and FREIDA FRISARO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Strongest winds in a decade to hit Southern California, bringing extreme fire risk
item.description: The strongest winds in more than a decade could hit Southern California, potentially causing structural damage and bringing extreme fire risk to areas that haven’t seen substantial rain in months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 30 years after discovery of remains, DNA could identify all of a serial killer’s victims
item.description: It's been decades since thousands of human bones and bone fragments were unearthed on a suspected Indiana serial killer’s property.
item.dc:creator: Rick Callahan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A 3-year-old boy's death in Honolulu fireworks explosion raises toll to 4
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A 3-year-old boy who died Monday was the fourth person to succumb to injuries from a massive explosion of fireworks on New Year's Eve at a Honolulu home, and a doctor said six others faced long recoveries in Arizona, where they were sent to be treated for severe burns.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's sentencing still on for Friday after judge rebuffs his push for a delay while he appeals
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump remains on track to be sentenced this week in his hush money case after a judge on Monday denied his request to halt proceedings while he appeals a ruling that upheld the historic verdict. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: U-Haul stolen in Albuquerque with Nine Inch Nails memorabilia inside
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (KRQE) – It was not the trip one man driving through Albuquerque expected: his U-Haul full of priceless items from one of his favorite bands was stolen. Ryan Jonik said he's out more than $50,000 from the items stolen, not to mention the sentimental value.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, NewsNation

item.title: Drugged-up driver leaves NY officer critically injured after running him off road while evading arrest: police
item.description: A Long Island, New York, man was arrested on Sunday after allegedly speeding down the highway in his Mustang while high on drugs, causing a crash that critically injured a police officer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly was in a 'Cabinet meeting' during Mar-a-lago visit
item.description: While Trump's approach is high-risk, O'Reilly argues it reflects his commitment to his agenda and disregard for political norms.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa Bay doctor sexually exploited kids, told them to 'hang themselves': DOJ
item.description: A palliative care doctor in Tampa Bay has been arrested after allegedly sexually exploiting several children and directing them to "cut, choke, and hang themselves," according to the U.S. Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis agrees to overhaul police training and force policies after George Floyd's murder
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — The Minneapolis City Council on Monday approved an agreement with the federal government to overhaul the city's police training and use-of-force policies in the wake of the murder of George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald's is the latest company to roll back diversity goals
item.description: Four years after launching a push for more diversity in its ranks, McDonald’s is ending some of its diversity practices, citing a U.S. Supreme Court decision that outlawed affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Self-help author James Arthur Ray, whose seminar led to tragedy and death, dies at 67
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — James Arthur Ray, a self-help guru whose multimillion-dollar business toppled after he led a sweat lodge ceremony in Arizona that left three people dead, has died. He was 67.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Boil water advisory issued for Richmond, Virginia, residents
item.description: "Some customers may experience a total loss of water service, while others may experience varying degrees of loss in water pressure," the city said in its release.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Nadeau, NewsNation

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, KRON4

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: The drama-free proceeding stood in stark contrast to four years ago when pro-Trump rioters violently stormed the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star allegedly choked, punched children's mother: police
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan, who was arrested last week in Myrtle Beach on a domestic violence charge, allegedly choked and punched the woman he lived with, according to a police report obtained by Nexstar's WBTW.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, Fox 5

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Zendaya and Tom Holland are engaged: Report
item.description: Speculation of a possible engagement started circulating online after Zendaya was seen at the Golden Globes Sunday wearing a massive diamond on her ring finger.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Pet owners on high alert after dog eats rodent poison in Denver neighborhood
item.description: “Always consider the potential risks to pets, wildlife, and children in your community," Animal Law Enforcement Sgt. Bryanna Munns said.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Biden issues ban on offshore oil and gas drilling in most federal waters. Trump vows to undo it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is moving to ban new offshore oil and gas drilling in most U.S. coastal waters, a last-minute effort to block possible action by the incoming Trump administration to expand offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral has started. Here's what to know
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter began Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds of animals killed in Dallas shopping center fire
item.description: A fire that broke out at a shopping center in Dallas on Friday morning killed more than 500 animals, most of which were small birds, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 39,000 John Deere mowers recalled
item.description: About 39,000 John Deere ZTrak™ Zero Turn Mowers with Kawasaki Engines have been recalled because the voltage regulator inside the mower's engine can fail while in use or while the product is being stored.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington will receive the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington will be awarded the nation's highest civilian honor on Saturday in a White House ceremony.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans attacker’s apparent loyalty to Islamic State group highlights persistent threat of lone wolf terrorism
item.description: The deadly Jan. 1, 2025, attack in New Orleans serves as a reminder of the persistent threat to the U.S. from individuals inspired by extremist Islamist groups.
item.dc:creator: Sara Harmouch, American University, Fox 5

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9

item.title: Surgeon general calls for cancer warnings on alcohol
item.description: Alcoholic beverages should carry a prominent warning label about the link between alcohol and cancer, according to the U.S. surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9
